description: The in-repo plugin source `plugins/trine-eval/skills/sprint-workflow/SKILL.md` documents only Steps 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 — it does not contain Step 0.5 (regression gate), Step 1d (tasks.json emission), or Step 3d (Batch API submission grouping). The Sprint 9 schema port added prose in `plugins/trine-eval/skills/sprint-contract/SKILL.md` at lines 74, 140, 142 referencing these three steps as if they exist in the in-repo workflow. The references resolve only against the cached/published v0.3.3 plugin from which actual harness-sprint execution loads — the in-repo source has silently diverged from the published artifact. Architect and scope-guardian independently confirmed the gap; orchestrator spot-checked via direct grep (logged to audit-log). Andon issued `alert` (not `stop`) because runtime execution against the cached SKILL.md is unaffected; the divergence is documentation correctness, not execution risk. This Henkaten captures the structural discovery: the repo-source workflow has been drifting from the published workflow without registration, and Sprint 9's port now creates dangling references that make the drift visible.

evidence.claim: In-repo sprint-workflow/SKILL.md defines exactly six steps (0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5) — no Step 0.5, no Step 1d, no Step 3d.
The Sprint 9 schema port wrote three references to non-existent in-repo workflow steps into sprint-contract/SKILL.md at lines 74, 140, 142.
The reference points to a published v0.3.3 cached harness-sprint/SKILL.md that the harness execution path uses — the in-repo source has not been ported to parity.
Sprint 9's declared Out-of-Scope forbids touching any SKILL.md other than sprint-contract/SKILL.md, so the gap cannot be closed inside Sprint 9 — it requires a follow-up scheduled sprint.

Architect (this swarm) issued andon `alert` (not `stop`) because the actual workflow execution loads from cached v0.3.3 SKILL.md which has the steps. Scope-guardian and henkaten-detector concurred. Orchestrator spot-checked the underlying grep claim and logged PASS to audit-log.

Classified as change_origin=passive because the gap has been present continuously since the v0.3.3 / repo-source divergence began; Sprint 9 did not introduce the gap, only surfaced it through new prose that references the missing steps.

andon_signal.reason: Documentation references in-repo workflow steps that do not exist in the in-repo workflow file; runtime execution against the cached v0.3.3 SKILL.md is unaffected, so this is documentation correctness, not execution risk. Resolved within takt budget — all three council agents (architect, scope-guardian, henkaten-detector) concurred alert is the correct level.
